theater_description: Federal entry into Asaba and the subsequent killing of civilians in town over several days, culminating in the Ogbe-Osowa massacre sequence on 7 October 1967.
geometry_method_note: Episode is bounded to Asaba town and not generalized to the whole Midwest front or all of Biafra.
military_area_note: No whole-country or whole-division denominator is used; actual combatants present in the town at the time of the massacre were not numerically recoverable at publishable quality.
civilian_area_note: Civilian denominator uses town-level population only and explicitly avoids national or region-wide populations.
split_reason: Separated from whole-war famine/blockade narratives because direct violent deaths can be identified more cleanly at Asaba than at the aggregate war scale.
quality_note: Civilian direct deaths are strongly supported as a floor, and town population is bounded, but combatant deaths/exposure remain too weak for a full DR.
